message.content: Only the projects page have the subtitle like `Tracked projects, releases, and urgency at a glance.` but other pages dont have it

content.text: Let me explore the frontend to understand the current page structure and identify which pages are missing subtitles.

input.prompt:
Explore the frontend in web/ directory. I need to find:

1. All page files (page.tsx) in the app directory
2. How the projects page displays its subtitle "Tracked projects, releases, and urgency at a glance."
3. Which other pages exist and whether they have subtitles

Look at the layout and page components to understand the pattern. Be thorough - check all page.tsx files and any shared layout/header components.

data.prompt:
Explore the frontend in web/ directory. I need to find:

1. All page files (page.tsx) in the app directory
2. How the projects page displays its subtitle "Tracked projects, releases, and urgency at a glance."
3. Which other pages exist and whether they have subtitles

Look at the layout and page components to understand the pattern. Be thorough - check all page.tsx files and any shared layout/header components.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings: ## Frontend Exploration Summary ### All Page Files Found I found the following page files in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/`: 1. **`/app/page.tsx`** - Dashboard (home page) 2. **`/app/projects/page.tsx`** - Projects listing 3. **`/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx`** - Project detail 4. **`/app/projects/[id]/agent/page.tsx`** - Agent configuration 5. **`/app/projects/[id]/semantic-releases/[srId]/page.tsx`** - Semantic release detail 6. **`/app/releases/page.tsx`** - Releases listing 7. **`/app/releases/[id]/page.tsx`** - Release detail 8. **`/app/semantic-releases/page.tsx`** - Redirects to `/releases` 9. **`/app/sources/page.tsx`** - Sources listing 10. **`/app/channels/page.tsx`** - Notification channels 11. **`/app/subscriptions/page.tsx`** - Subscriptions management 12. **`/app/onboard/page.tsx`** - Quick onboard/dependency scanning 13. **`/app/todo/page.tsx`** - Todo items management 14. **`/app/login/page.tsx`** - Login page ### Projects Page Subtitle Pattern The projects page displays its subtitle as: **From `/app/projects/page.tsx` (lines 598-606):** ```typescript

{t("projects.title")}

{t("projects.description")}

``` **From `/web/lib/i18n/messages/en.json` (line 210):** ```json "projects.description": "Tracked projects, releases, and urgency at a glance." ``` The subtitle is rendered as a `

` tag with: - CSS class: `mt-1 text-[13px] text-text-secondary` - Font family: `var(--font-dm-sans)` (DM Sans) - It includes an `` component (lines 145-186) that provides a hover tooltip ### Pages with Subtitles **Pages that have subtitles:** 1. **`/projects`** - "Tracked projects, releases, and urgency at a glance." 2. **`/sources`** - "Ingestion sources across all projects. Sources are managed within their project." (lines 56-61) 3. **`/onboard`** - "Scan a GitHub repository to detect dependencies and start tracking their releases." (lines 171-176) **Pages without subtitles:** 1. **`/` (Dashboard)** - Only has title "Dashboard" 2. **`/releases`** - Only has title "Releases" 3. **`/semantic-releases`** - Redirects to `/releases` 4. **`/channels`** - Only has title "Channels" 5. **`/subscriptions`** - Only has title "Subscriptions" 6. **`/todo`** - Only has title "Todo" 7. **`/login`** - Custom layout, no subtitle ### Layout Components The main layout structure uses: - **Root layout:** `/app/layout.tsx` - Sets up fonts (Fraunces and DM Sans), providers, and auth context - **Layout shell:** Used via `` component in the root layout - Font variables exported: `--font-fraunces` and `--font-dm-sans` ### Subtitle Pattern Implementation Pages with subtitles follow this consistent pattern: ```typescript

Let me compile my findings: ## Frontend Exploration Summary ### All Page Files Found I found the following page files in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/`: 1. **`/app/page.tsx`** - Dashboard (home page) 2. **`/app/projects/page.tsx`** - Projects listing 3. **`/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx`** - Project detail 4. **`/app/projects/[id]/agent/page.tsx`** - Agent configuration 5. **`/app/projects/[id]/semantic-releases/[srId]/page.tsx`** - Semantic release detail 6. **`/app/releases/page.tsx`** - Releases listing 7. **`/app/releases/[id]/page.tsx`** - Release detail 8. **`/app/semantic-releases/page.tsx`** - Redirects to `/releases` 9. **`/app/sources/page.tsx`** - Sources listing 10. **`/app/channels/page.tsx`** - Notification channels 11. **`/app/subscriptions/page.tsx`** - Subscriptions management 12. **`/app/onboard/page.tsx`** - Quick onboard/dependency scanning 13. **`/app/todo/page.tsx`** - Todo items management 14. **`/app/login/page.tsx`** - Login page ### Projects Page Subtitle Pattern The projects page displays its subtitle as: **From `/app/projects/page.tsx` (lines 598-606):** ```typescript

{t("projects.title")}

{t("projects.description")}

``` **From `/web/lib/i18n/messages/en.json` (line 210):** ```json "projects.description": "Tracked projects, releases, and urgency at a glance." ``` The subtitle is rendered as a `

` tag with: - CSS class: `mt-1 text-[13px] text-text-secondary` - Font family: `var(--font-dm-sans)` (DM Sans) - It includes an `` component (lines 145-186) that provides a hover tooltip ### Pages with Subtitles **Pages that have subtitles:** 1. **`/projects`** - "Tracked projects, releases, and urgency at a glance." 2. **`/sources`** - "Ingestion sources across all projects. Sources are managed within their project." (lines 56-61) 3. **`/onboard`** - "Scan a GitHub repository to detect dependencies and start tracking their releases." (lines 171-176) **Pages without subtitles:** 1. **`/` (Dashboard)** - Only has title "Dashboard" 2. **`/releases`** - Only has title "Releases" 3. **`/semantic-releases`** - Redirects to `/releases` 4. **`/channels`** - Only has title "Channels" 5. **`/subscriptions`** - Only has title "Subscriptions" 6. **`/todo`** - Only has title "Todo" 7. **`/login`** - Custom layout, no subtitle ### Layout Components The main layout structure uses: - **Root layout:** `/app/layout.tsx` - Sets up fonts (Fraunces and DM Sans), providers, and auth context - **Layout shell:** Used via `` component in the root layout - Font variables exported: `--font-fraunces` and `--font-dm-sans` ### Subtitle Pattern Implementation Pages with subtitles follow this consistent pattern: ```typescript

{t("PAGE.title")}

{t("PAGE.description")}

``` This is a straightforward pattern where each page with a subtitle uses `t()` (translation) to pull the description from i18n messages.
